translational: Supports integrated regulation of endocannabinoid and endocannabinoid-like lipid signalling through precursor availability, N-acyl ethanolamine production, degradation pathways, and neuromodulatory interactions influencing motivation, stress responsiveness, neuroinflammatory regulation, and cognitive stability. This functional state focuses on diet-actionable endocannabinoidome biology — NAEs (N-acyl ethanolamides, including PEA and OEA), phospholipid precursors, omega-3-derived ethanolamides (DHEA and EPEA), FAAH preservation, endocannabinoid–dopamine interactions, and stress-buffering pathways — rather than direct pharmacological activation of CB1 or CB2 receptors.
scientific: Integrated regulation of endocannabinoid and endocannabinoid-like lipid signalling through NAPE/NAE biosynthesis, omega-3-derived ethanolamide production, FAAH-mediated preservation, dopaminergic neuromodulation, and stress-buffering capacity — framed as diet-actionable endocannabinoidome biology rather than CB1/CB2 receptor pharmacology.
bullets: Supports motivation, stress buffering, and neuromodulatory lipid signalling through the endocannabinoidome — within BRS-X.
Links phospholipid and omega-3 substrate context to endogenous NAE biology — Supporting BRS1.
Buffers stress responsiveness through HPA-axis and neuroinflammatory interaction — Supporting BRS6.
subtitle: (Endocannabinoidome Signalling & Neuromodulation)

translational: Governs signalling lipids that act as neuromodulators throughout the nervous system, helping the body adapt to stress, regulate motivation, and maintain physiological balance. It encompasses generation of N-acyl phosphatidylethanolamines (NAPEs) and their downstream N-acyl ethanolamines (NAEs), including anandamide (AEA), palmitoylethanolamide (PEA), and oleoylethanolamide (OEA).
scientific: Biosynthesis capacity for NAPEs and downstream NAEs (including AEA, PEA, and OEA) — governing endocannabinoidome signalling lipids that modulate stress adaptation, motivation, and physiological balance.
bullets: Generates NAPE and NAE signalling lipids including AEA, PEA, and OEA — within BRS-X.
Depends on phospholipid precursor availability from dietary input — within BRS-X.
Supports neuromodulatory capacity influencing motivation and stress adaptation — within BRS-X.

translational: Supports production of omega-3-derived ethanolamides and related signalling molecules, including DHEA and EPEA, from EPA and DHA availability (linking dietary omega-3 status to endocannabinoidome neuromodulation).
scientific: Production of omega-3-derived ethanolamides and related signalling molecules including DHEA and EPEA from EPA and DHA availability.
bullets: Produces omega-3-derived ethanolamides DHEA and EPEA from EPA and DHA — within BRS-X.
Links long-chain omega-3 intake to endocannabinoidome neuromodulation — Supporting BRS1.
Depends on membrane phospholipid and fatty-acid substrate context — Supporting BRS2.

translational: Regulates fatty acid amide hydrolase (FAAH) activity, influencing how long anandamide and related NAEs persist before degradation (endocannabinoid preservation through reduced breakdown).
scientific: Regulation of fatty acid amide hydrolase (FAAH) activity influencing the persistence and degradation of anandamide and related NAEs.
bullets: Preserves anandamide and related NAEs by modulating FAAH degradation — within BRS-X.
Extends neuromodulatory signalling duration without receptor pharmacology — within BRS-X.
Supports stress-buffering and motivational neuromodulation downstream — within BRS-X.

translational: Describes interaction between endocannabinoid signalling and dopaminergic reward, motivation, and behavioural activation pathways (linking endocannabinoidome biology to drive and effort-related neurocircuitry).
scientific: Interaction between endocannabinoid signalling and dopaminergic reward, motivation, and behavioural activation pathways.
bullets: Modulates dopaminergic reward and behavioural activation pathways — Supporting BRS1.
Links endocannabinoidome signalling to motivation and drive context — within BRS-X.
Operates through neuromodulatory interaction rather than direct receptor stimulation — within BRS-X.

translational: Supports endocannabinoid-mediated buffering of stress responsiveness through interactions with neuroinflammatory pathways, HPA-axis activity (hypothalamic–pituitary–adrenal stress hormone regulation), and excitatory neurotransmission.
scientific: Endocannabinoid-mediated buffering of stress responsiveness through interactions with neuroinflammatory pathways, HPA-axis activity, and excitatory neurotransmission.
bullets: Buffers stress responsiveness through endocannabinoid-mediated neuromodulation — within BRS-X.
Intersects HPA-axis and neuroinflammatory stress pathways — Supporting BRS6.
Modulates excitatory neurotransmission under stress load — Supporting BRS1.

translational: Helps ensure availability of phosphatidylethanolamine and phospholipid precursors required for NAPE biosynthesis and downstream N-acyl ethanolamine (NAE) production within the diet-actionable endocannabinoidome.
scientific: Availability of phosphatidylethanolamine and phospholipid precursors required for NAPE biosynthesis and downstream N-acyl ethanolamine (NAE) production within the diet-actionable endocannabinoidome.
bullets: Constrains NAPE and NAE biosynthesis when phospholipid precursors are insufficient — within BRS-X.
Links dietary phospholipid intake to endocannabinoidome capacity — within BRS-X.
Intersects membrane phospholipid chemistry represented in BRS2 — Supporting BRS2.

translational: Supports integrated regulation of oestrogen, progesterone, gut-mediated sex-hormone metabolism, and metabolic–reproductive hormone signalling, influencing neurocognitive stability through coordinated hormone signalling, enterohepatic recycling, microbial metabolism, and insulin-linked endocrine regulation. Circulating sex hormones are bidirectionally coupled to gut microbial ecology via the estrobolome (microbiome-mediated oestrogen metabolism and recycling).
scientific: Integrated regulation of oestrogen, progesterone, gut-mediated sex-hormone metabolism, and metabolic–reproductive hormone signalling — influencing neurocognitive stability through coordinated hormone signalling, enterohepatic recycling, microbial metabolism, and insulin-linked endocrine regulation.
bullets: Coordinates oestrogen, progesterone, and androgen signalling with neurocognitive context — within BRS-X.
Links gut estrobolome and microbial metabolism to sex-hormone exposure — Supporting BRS5.
Integrates insulin and metabolic signals with reproductive hormone balance — Supporting BRS6.
subtitle: (Reproductive Hormone Balance & Neurocognitive Regulation)

translational: Regulates oestrogen-mediated signalling relevant to dopamine tone, cognitive stability, emotional regulation, and menstrual/perimenopausal ADHD symptom fluctuation. This mechanism describes oestrogen-linked neural signalling context — not diagnosis, treatment efficacy, or deterministic ADHD phenome claims.
scientific: Regulation of oestrogen-mediated signalling relevant to dopamine tone, cognitive stability, emotional regulation, and menstrual/perimenopausal ADHD symptom fluctuation.
bullets: Modulates oestrogen-linked dopamine tone and cognitive stability context — Supporting BRS1.
Frames menstrual and perimenopausal symptom fluctuation without diagnostic claims — within BRS-X.
Intersects emotional regulation biology across reproductive life stages — within BRS-X.

translational: Supports microbiome-mediated regulation of oestrogen metabolism, deconjugation, recycling, and elimination through beta-glucuronidase activity and enterohepatic circulation (the estrobolome pathway linking gut ecology to systemic oestrogen exposure).
scientific: Microbiome-mediated regulation of oestrogen metabolism, deconjugation, recycling, and elimination through beta-glucuronidase activity and enterohepatic circulation.
bullets: Regulates oestrogen deconjugation and enterohepatic recycling via the estrobolome — within BRS-X.
Links gut microbial ecology to systemic oestrogen exposure — Supporting BRS5.
Shapes downstream oestrogen signalling stability represented in sibling PMs — within BRS-X.

translational: Supports microbial and metabolite-linked stability of progesterone-related hormonal balance, potentially involving butyrate-producing organisms such as Faecalibacterium prausnitzii and Roseburia (gut ecology contributing to reproductive hormone context).
scientific: Microbial and metabolite-linked support for progesterone-related hormonal stability, potentially involving butyrate-producing organisms such as Faecalibacterium prausnitzii and Roseburia.
bullets: Supports progesterone-related hormonal stability through microbial metabolite context — within BRS-X.
Links butyrate-producing guilds to reproductive endocrine balance — Supporting BRS5.
Contributes to coordinated sex-hormone regulation across the parent FM — within BRS-X.

translational: Coordinates insulin regulation, metabolic signalling, gut barrier and microbial function, and reproductive hormone balance — including mechanisms through which Akkermansia muciniphila and glycaemic–insulin stability may influence oestrogen–progesterone harmony.
scientific: Coordination between insulin regulation, metabolic signalling, gut barrier/microbial function, and reproductive hormone balance, including mechanisms through which Akkermansia muciniphila and glycaemic-insulin stability may influence oestrogen-progesterone harmony.
bullets: Integrates insulin and metabolic signals with reproductive hormone balance — Supporting BRS6.
Links gut barrier and keystone taxa to endocrine harmony — Supporting BRS5.
Coordinates oestrogen–progesterone context across metabolic and microbial inputs — within BRS-X.

translational: Supports integrated regulation of testosterone availability and androgen receptor signalling influencing behavioural activation, motivation, mental stamina, persistence, and goal-directed effort. Testosterone intersects dopaminergic motivation circuits, effort allocation, and fatigue vulnerability where androgen exposure is low.
scientific: Integrated regulation of testosterone availability and androgen receptor signalling influencing behavioural activation, motivation, mental stamina, persistence, and goal-directed effort.
bullets: Modulates androgen receptor signalling relevant to motivation and effort allocation — within BRS-X.
Intersects dopaminergic motivation circuits where androgen exposure is low — Supporting BRS1.
Supports behavioural activation and goal-directed effort context — within BRS-X.

translational: Supports microbiome-mediated regulation of androgen metabolism, degradation, recycling, and systemic androgen exposure through microbial steroid-transforming enzymes and enterohepatic circulation.
scientific: Microbiome-mediated regulation of androgen metabolism, degradation, recycling and systemic androgen exposure through microbial steroid-transforming enzymes and enterohepatic circulation.
bullets: Regulates androgen metabolism and enterohepatic recycling via microbial enzymes — within BRS-X.
Links gut ecology to systemic androgen exposure — Supporting BRS5.
Shapes testosterone signalling stability represented in sibling PMs — within BRS-X.

translational: Supports the body's ability to recycle homocysteine (a sulfur amino acid that rises when one-carbon metabolism is strained), produce SAMe (S-adenosylmethionine, the body's universal methyl donor), and sustain methylation capacity across neurotransmitter synthesis, membrane chemistry, and gene regulation. Efficient one-carbon cycling is a foundation for many brain-relevant processes.
scientific: Integrated regulation of folate/B12-dependent remethylation, betaine/BHMT remethylation, SAMe synthesis, and methionine cycle flux — together sustaining homocysteine recycling, methyl-donor output, and methylation capacity across neurotransmitter, membrane, epigenetic, and repair chemistry.
bullets: Sustains neurotransmitter synthesis and monoaminergic pathway support — Supporting BRS1.
Helps control homocysteine-linked oxidative and inflammatory burden — Supporting BRS3.
Supports mitochondrial function, epigenetic regulation, and cellular repair — Supporting BRS4.
subtitle: (One-Carbon Methylation & Homocysteine Recycling)

translational: Maintains the body's methylation capacity by recycling homocysteine back into methionine through folate- and vitamin B12-dependent remethylation (the primary one-carbon route for clearing homocysteine). This helps sustain the methyl-donor supply required for many brain and body processes while limiting excessive homocysteine accumulation.
scientific: Conversion of homocysteine back to methionine through folate- and B12-dependent remethylation — the primary dietary-actionable route for sustaining methylation capacity and limiting homocysteine accumulation.
bullets: Supports neurotransmitter synthesis and regulation through methylation-dependent pathways — Supporting BRS1.
Helps limit oxidative stress and inflammatory burden associated with elevated homocysteine — Supporting BRS3.
Contributes to mitochondrial resilience, cellular repair and metabolic function — Supporting BRS4.

translational: Provides a parallel route for clearing homocysteine and keeping methyl donors available when folate-pathway demands are high or constrained, via betaine-dependent remethylation through the BHMT enzyme (betaine–homocysteine methyltransferase). Betaine- and choline-rich dietary patterns help maintain methylation resilience under increased one-carbon load.
scientific: Alternative betaine-dependent recycling of homocysteine to methionine through the BHMT pathway — sustaining methionine and SAMe supply when primary folate remethylation is under strain.
bullets: Supports continued methionine and SAMe supply when primary folate remethylation is under strain — within BRS2.
Helps sustain phospholipid and membrane methylation chemistry downstream — Supporting BRS1.
Contributes to homocysteine control linked to oxidative and inflammatory stress — Supporting BRS3.

translational: Converts regenerated methionine into SAMe (S-adenosylmethionine, the body's universal methyl donor), powering methylation reactions across brain and body. Without adequate SAMe production, downstream methylation chemistry cannot proceed effectively.
scientific: Production of S-adenosylmethionine from methionine to supply universal methyl donation across neurotransmitter, membrane, epigenetic, and repair reactions.
bullets: Enables neurotransmitter-related methylation and monoamine pathway support — Supporting BRS1.
Supplies methyl groups for phospholipid and membrane chemistry — Supporting BRS1.
Supports epigenetic regulation and broader cellular repair processes — Supporting BRS4.

translational: Reflects how efficiently the whole methionine cycle runs — affecting homocysteine levels, SAMe output, and overall methylation capacity across the system. Effective cycle throughput depends on dietary patterns rather than any single nutrient in isolation.
scientific: Integrated throughput of the methionine cycle governing homocysteine clearance, SAMe output, and system-wide methylation capacity — dependent on coordinated substrate and cofactor sufficiency rather than isolated nutrient intake.
bullets: Governs how well homocysteine is cleared and methyl donors are replenished — within BRS2.
Influences neurotransmitter and membrane methylation capacity downstream — Supporting BRS1.
Links one-carbon efficiency to mitochondrial and metabolic resilience — Supporting BRS4.

translational: Provides an alternative path for handling homocysteine by converting it toward cysteine through transsulfuration (the sulfur-branch of one-carbon metabolism), linking one-carbon metabolism to sulfur amino acid supply. This branch helps balance remethylation demand with antioxidant precursor availability.
scientific: Conversion of homocysteine toward cysteine through the transsulfuration pathway — balancing remethylation demand with sulfur amino acid and glutathione precursor supply.
bullets: Feeds cysteine supply for glutathione production and cellular redox protection — within BRS2.
Helps limit oxidative stress associated with homocysteine accumulation — Supporting BRS3.
Supports detoxification and cellular defence capacity under metabolic load — Supporting BRS3.

translational: Helps the body build glutathione (a central intracellular antioxidant) from sulfur amino acids supplied through the methylation network. Glutathione synthesis connects one-carbon handling to everyday cellular protection against oxidative damage.
scientific: Synthesis of glutathione from cysteine and related sulfur amino acids supplied through the methylation–transsulfuration network — linking one-carbon metabolism to cellular redox protection.
bullets: Supports limiting oxidative stress and inflammatory burden at the cellular level — Supporting BRS3.
Helps protect mitochondrial and neuronal tissue from redox strain — Supporting BRS4.
Contributes to resilience against homocysteine-linked metabolic stress — within BRS2.

translational: Links homocysteine handling to cysteine supply and glutathione production (the body's principal cellular antioxidant), connecting methylation metabolism to antioxidant defence. This integrated state helps the body convert one-carbon stress into redox resilience.
scientific: Integrated coupling of transsulfuration pathway flux and glutathione synthesis — linking homocysteine handling to cysteine supply and cellular redox defence within BRS2.
bullets: Builds glutathione-based protection against oxidative and inflammatory damage — Supporting BRS3.
Supports cellular and mitochondrial defence under metabolic strain — Supporting BRS4.
Helps clear homocysteine through alternative sulfur-amino-acid pathways — within BRS2.
subtitle: (Transsulfuration, Cysteine Supply & Glutathione Defence)

translational: Connects methylation capacity to membrane phospholipid chemistry — the bridge between B-vitamin status and the membrane environment the brain relies on. SAMe supply and phosphatidylcholine formation work together to shape omega-3 carriage and membrane integrity.
scientific: Integrated coupling of SAMe-dependent methylation and phosphatidylcholine formation — linking one-carbon metabolism to neuronal membrane phospholipid chemistry and omega-3 carriage.
bullets: Supports neuronal membrane composition and long-chain fat delivery to the brain — Supporting BRS1.
Links one-carbon and B-vitamin nutrition to membrane signalling competence — Supporting BRS1.
Depends on methylation efficiency interacting with omega-3 status for full effect — within BRS2.
subtitle: (Methylation–Membrane Phospholipid Coupling)

translational: Helps build phospholipid membranes that carry omega-3 fatty acids toward the brain, supporting membrane flexibility and cell signalling. This connects methylation capacity and B-vitamin status to the structural lipid environment in which neural signalling occurs.
scientific: Formation of phosphatidylcholine from methylation-dependent phospholipid chemistry — supporting membrane structure, omega-3 carriage, and neuronal signalling competence.
bullets: Supports long-chain omega-3 delivery and neuronal membrane DHA enrichment — Supporting BRS1.
Links B-vitamin and methylation status to membrane phospholipid chemistry — within BRS2.
Contributes to receptor function and signalling competence at the membrane surface — Supporting BRS1.

translational: Helps ensure the body has enough methyl-group donors to sustain homocysteine recycling, SAMe production, and the many methylation reactions required for brain function, cellular maintenance, and metabolic regulation. Depletion of this shared pool can constrain multiple pathways simultaneously.
scientific: Shared one-carbon donor pool constraining folate/B12-dependent remethylation, betaine/BHMT remethylation, SAMe synthesis, and downstream methylation reactions across BRS2 mechanisms.
bullets: Underpins neurotransmitter and monoamine pathway methylation support — Supporting BRS1.
Constrains membrane phospholipid methylation and omega-3 carriage — within BRS2.
Influences epigenetic regulation, repair, and metabolic adaptation — Supporting BRS4.

translational: Helps ensure sufficient sulfur amino acid and methionine substrates to sustain methylation cycling, transsulfuration, and glutathione production. This shared pool supports both methyl-group metabolism and antioxidant defence.
scientific: Shared methionine and transsulfuration substrate pool sustaining methylation cycling, transsulfuration flux, and glutathione synthesis across BRS2 mechanisms.
bullets: Feeds methionine cycling and SAMe production across BRS2 — within BRS2.
Supplies transsulfuration substrates for glutathione and redox protection — Supporting BRS3.
Supports mitochondrial and cellular repair under sulfur-amino-acid demand — Supporting BRS4.

translational: Helps explain why inherited variation in one-carbon metabolism genes may alter folate utilisation, methyl-donor efficiency, homocysteine regulation, and responsiveness to nutritional support — a variant-sensitive interpretation of BRS2 biology without changing underlying PM mechanisms.
scientific: Inherited variation in MTHFR, MTR/MTRR, BHMT, CBS, PEMT, and related one-carbon genes may modulate folate remethylation, SAMe generation, transsulfuration, and phospholipid methylation efficiency — overlaying variant-sensitive interpretation on BRS2(FM1–FM3) biology without deterministic outcome claims.
bullets: Modulates how folate-dependent remethylation, SAMe synthesis, and transsulfuration are interpreted — within BRS2.
May influence downstream neurotransmitter and phospholipid pathways through altered methyl-donor availability — Supporting BRS1 and BRS4.
Provides context for inter-individual nutritional responsiveness without deterministic genetic outcomes.
